title: Tamale Pie
intro: Tamale pie is an early 20th-century American casserole inspired by Mexican tamales and chili con carne. It became popular in Texas, California, and the Midwest as a practical oven-baked alternative to labor-intensive corn-husk tamales, layering chili-style filling with cornmeal in a single family-style dish. This EWHF version keeps the chile-corn backbone while replacing the traditional beef filling with vegetables and legumes, preserving the spirit of adaptable Tex-Mex comfort food.
ingredients: ¼ cup brown or green lentils
1 can (10 oz) Ro-Tel diced tomatoes with green chiles
½ cup shredded low-fat sharp cheddar
steps: Place ¾ cup dried black beans in a bowl and cover with at least 3 inches of cold water. Soak 8 to 12 hours at room temperature or up to 24 hours refrigerated. Drain.

Transfer the beans to a pot and cover with cold water by about 2 inches. Bring to a boil, reduce to a gentle simmer, and cook uncovered 75 to 95 minutes until fully tender. Drain and reserve ½ cup of the cooking liquid. Spread beans on a sheet pan and let steam dry 5 to 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F using Convection Bake.

In a bowl stir ¾ cup yellow cornmeal into 1 cup cold water to make a slurry. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a saucepan. Slowly pour in the slurry while stirring. Add 1 tablespoon olive oil and ½ teaspoon salt. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook 30 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes. Thin with hot water if needed. Let rest 10 minutes.
Rinse ¼ cup lentils and place in a saucepan with 1¼ cups cold water. Bring to a boil then simmer 18 minutes until tender but intact. Drain and let steam dry 5 to 30 minutes.
Preheat a 12-inch skillet over medium heat.
Prepare mise en place: mince 4 oz mushrooms and chop the remaining 4 oz; chop onions; finely chop celery; mince garlic; measure chili powder, cumin, and smoked paprika; drain the diced green chiles.
Add 1 teaspoon olive oil to the skillet and add mushrooms. Cook 8 to 10 minutes until moisture releases and cooks off, then cook 2 to 3 minutes more until lightly browned.
Add onions and celery, season with salt and black pepper, and cook about 5 minutes until softened.
Add plant-based sausage and cook about 4 minutes, breaking it into small crumbles as it browns.
Add garlic, chili powder, cumin, and smoked paprika and cook about 45 seconds until fragrant.
Reduce heat to medium-low. Add cooked black beans and mash about one quarter of them in the pan. Stir in the cooked lentils.
Add the Ro-Tel tomatoes with chiles, the diced green chiles, and the frozen corn. Add 2 tablespoons reserved bean cooking liquid.
Simmer about 6 minutes over medium-low heat. Taste and adjust salt if needed. Remove from heat.
Spoon about two-thirds of the polenta into a baking dish and spread across the bottom, pushing it slightly up the sides to form a shallow lining.
Add the filling and spread evenly.
In a small bowl combine refried beans, 1 tablespoon Greek yogurt, 1 tablespoon Parmigiano-Reggiano, and 1 tablespoon warm water. Stir until smooth and spread evenly over the filling.
Spread the remaining polenta over the top without pressing down.
Scatter sliced black olives over the surface.
Mix the shredded cheddar, remaining Greek yogurt, and remaining Parmigiano-Reggiano and spread evenly over the top.
Bake at 350°F convection for 45 minutes until bubbling at the edges and lightly browned.
Let rest 10 minutes before serving.
